name: Chuck 70 Hi × CDG PLAY
description: The Chuck 70 rebuilt for Comme des Garçons PLAY: heavier canvas, a glossy egret midsole and Filip Pagowski's peeking heart printed on the lateral panel. The 1970s last, the diamond outsole, and the one detail everybody recognises from across the street.

name: Air Jordan 1 High Element GORE-TEX
description: The 1985 silhouette built for weather it was never meant to see. A GORE-TEX shell with sealed seams over nubuck and ripstop, the word stamped across the heel in case anyone doubts it. Made for the walk home in the rain.

name: Air Jordan 4 Retro Alternate 89
description: The alternate that never made it to the floor in 1989, finally retro'd in 2016 and never brought back. White leather, gym red mesh under the wings, and a visible Air unit in the heel. Chicago colours without the Chicago noise.

name: Dunk Low Retro
description: Black leather across the toe and collar, anthracite suede overlays, and a metallic silver Swoosh that picks up every light in the room. The 1985 college hoops sole, kept low and kept quiet.

name: Forum Bonega
description: The 1984 Forum, lifted. A chunky platform midsole under a full-grain leather upper, fuzzy terry Three Stripes on the sidewall and a gold foil trefoil at the heel.

name: Air Jordan 1 Low × Travis Scott
description: The backwards Swoosh, this time in phantom over black nubuck, with university red hiding on the Jumpman and the tongue tag. Cactus Jack at the heel and a sail midsole that looks broken in before you open the box.

name: Chuck Taylor All Star Hi
description: The 1917 basketball shoe that quit basketball. Cotton canvas, a vulcanised rubber sole, the ankle patch, and nothing else. It gets better looking as it falls apart.

name: Old Skool
description: The first Vans to carry the Sidestripe — drawn by Paul Van Doren in 1977 and never redrawn since. Port royale suede and canvas, a padded collar, and the waffle outsole that built the brand.
